original_abstract: Porous heterogeneous catalysts are cornerstones of modern industrial chemistry. In this work, we report the synthesis and application of Hf-NU-1000, a novel hafnium-based metal-organic framework featuring Hf6 nodes. This material exhibits exceptional catalytic activity in the activation of epoxides, enabling the quantitative chemical fixation of CO2 into five-membered cyclic carbonates under ambient conditions. Beyond carbon capture, Hf-NU-1000 serves as a versatile multifunctional catalyst for a variety of epoxide transformations. Specifically, it facilitates the regioselective and enantioretentive formation of 1,2-bifunctionalized systems through solvolytic nucleophilic ring-opening reactions, highlighting its potential for precise organic synthesis.
humanized_abstract: Porous heterogeneous catalysts represent one of the most significant components in modern industrial chemistry. Herein, we demonstrate an effective way to synthesize a new hafnium-based metal-organic framework, Hf-NU-1000 with Hf6 clusters. As a proof of concept, the catalytic performance toward activation of epoxides and subsequent utilization in CO2-to-cyclic carbonates conversion as well as various other transformations were explored. Notably, quantitative CO2 fixation into five-membered cyclic carbonates is easily achieved within one atmosphere by employing only a small amount of catalyst. Meanwhile, Hf-NU-1000 could also be applied for the regioselective and enantioselective synthesis of 1,2-bifunctional products by means of solvolytic nucleophilic ring-opening of corresponding strained cyclic molecules, demonstrating its significant application in the precision organic transformation.
